proof: function[T0, T1](x0: ChartedSpace[T0, T1]) { is_charted_space_data[T0, T1](x0.atlas, x0.chart_at) }[M, E](c)
function[T0, T1](x0: Set[Chart[T0, T1]], x1: T0 -> Chart[T0, T1]) { ChartedSpace.constraint[T0, T1](x0, x1) = is_charted_space_data[T0, T1](x0, x1) }[M, E](c.atlas, c.chart_at)

goal: is_charted_space_data[M, E](c.atlas, c.chart_at) = (forall(x0: M) { c.chart_at(x0).source.contains(x0) } and forall(x1: M) { c.atlas.contains(c.chart_at(x1)) })
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> Chart[T0, T1], x1: Set[Chart[T0, T1]]) { (forall(x2: T0) { x2 ∈ x0(x2).source } and forall(x3: T0) { x0(x3) ∈ x1 }) = is_charted_space_data[T0, T1](x1, x0) }[M, E](c.chart_at, c.atlas)
function[T0, T1](x0: ChartedSpace[T0, T1]) { is_charted_space_data[T0, T1](x0.atlas, x0.chart_at) }[M, E](c)
not is_charted_space_data[M, E](c.atlas, c.chart_at) or (forall(x0: M) { x0 ∈ c.chart_at(x0).source } and forall(x1: M) { c.chart_at(x1) ∈ c.atlas })
